scenario_content:
[Issue Title]: TextSecure incorrectly thinks recipient is registered

[Post 1]:
Over the past week I've experienced a very strange failure mode.

Me and person P were exhanging unsecured text messages. I was using TextSecure (push registered), while P was using a stock messaging app.
At some point, TextSecure on my phone started thinking that P was registered with TextSecure, and switched to sending encrypted messages. I sent a few encrypted messages to P, which P never received.
Upon learning that P wasn't receiving my messages, I tried ending the secure session with P on my phone. This had no effect: TextSecure still insisted on sending encrypted messages. I tried de-registering my phone and then registering it again, but that didn't help. Ultimately the only way to reach P was to de-register my phone and keep push disabled :(

The most pressing issue is: Why did TextSecure think P was registered? P never installed TextSecure or Signal, and did not change their phone number, or anything like that.
The secondary issue is: Why did TextSecure not allow me to send plain SMS, even after I selected "end secure session"?
I looked at the log, and the only potentially relevant line I could find was this:
W/Directory(22806): Adding active token: +*********xx, 47+CqoNE/Fv5gw
(where xx is the last 2 digits of P's phone number, so I know the line corresponds to their account.)
(PS. Is the debug log designed to be safe for posting in public, or is there a document describing which fields should be censored?)
Version info for my device:
Device  : LGE Nexus 4 (occam)
Android : 5.1.1 (1836172, LMY47V)
Memory  : 33M (0.13% used, 192M max)
Memclass: 192
OS Host : wpiw7.hot.corp.google.com
App     : TextSecure 2.16.4

[Post 2]:
Why did TextSecure think P was registered? P never installed TextSecure or Signal

Does the WS unregister page work for P? https://whispersystems.org/textsecure/unregister/
If not, P is probably registered with Cyanogenmod's Whisperpush service.

[Post 3]:
How long does P use this number? It could be that the previous owner of the number registered and didn't deregister when the number expired. In that case the unregister link above would also be the answer.

[Post 4]:
After looking into this further, it is true that P had an old phone that used Cyanogenmod. The unregister link @brumsoel sent says "your number is registered with a different server", which suggests that P is indeed register with Cyanogenmod's Whisperpush. AFAICT there is no easy way to unregister from that (see CyanogenMod/android_external_whispersystems_WhisperPush#8), so I guess we're at a dead end for now. I'm closing this since the issue is more with Cyanogenmod than with TextSecure.

[Post 5]:
@cberzan did you try their unregister app? https://plus.google.com/+CyanogenMod/posts/ZdP44tAE9J3
or does it only work on the registered phone itself?

[Post 6]:
Ha! Apparently, there is a website for unregistering. Seems like no one found it yet?
https://whispertool.cyanogenmod.org/ (you'll get a warning about an expired SSL cert, dunno if the thing actually works)

[Post 7]:
I've tried both and they don't seem to work. Anyone have any other suggestions?

[Post 8]:
FYI, someone from CyanogenMod confirmed that https://whispertool.cyanogenmod.org/ is the tool to use for unregistering:
https://twitter.com/CyanogenMod/status/618580635804954624
You may need to wait a couple of hours for your un-registration to propagate to the Whispersystems servers before registering with Textsecure.
system_prompt:
你是一位经验丰富的软件工程师和项目管理者，专注于软件系统中的价值风险分析。
你的任务是分析给定的GitHub Issue讨论内容，识别其中涉及的人类价值关切。

价值风险是指软件系统可能对用户或利益相关者的重要价值（如隐私、安全、公平性、可用性等）产生负面影响的潜在问题。
Issue讨论中可能包含用户对软件行为的不满、功能请求、Bug报告等，这些内容可能隐含对某些价值的关切。
user_prompt:
## 价值场景（Issue讨论）
[Issue Title]: TextSecure incorrectly thinks recipient is registered

[Post 1]:
Over the past week I've experienced a very strange failure mode.

Me and person P were exhanging unsecured text messages. I was using TextSecure (push registered), while P was using a stock messaging app.
At some point, TextSecure on my phone started thinking that P was registered with TextSecure, and switched to sending encrypted messages. I sent a few encrypted messages to P, which P never received.
Upon learning that P wasn't receiving my messages, I tried ending the secure session with P on my phone. This had no effect: TextSecure still insisted on sending encrypted messages. I tried de-registering my phone and then registering it again, but that didn't help. Ultimately the only way to reach P was to de-register my phone and keep push disabled :(

The most pressing issue is: Why did TextSecure think P was registered? P never installed TextSecure or Signal, and did not change their phone number, or anything like that.
The secondary issue is: Why did TextSecure not allow me to send plain SMS, even after I selected "end secure session"?
I looked at the log, and the only potentially relevant line I could find was this:
W/Directory(22806): Adding active token: +*********xx, 47+CqoNE/Fv5gw
(where xx is the last 2 digits of P's phone number, so I know the line corresponds to their account.)
(PS. Is the debug log designed to be safe for posting in public, or is there a document describing which fields should be censored?)
Version info for my device:
Device  : LGE Nexus 4 (occam)
Android : 5.1.1 (1836172, LMY47V)
Memory  : 33M (0.13% used, 192M max)
Memclass: 192
OS Host : wpiw7.hot.corp.google.com
App     : TextSecure 2.16.4

[Post 2]:
Why did TextSecure think P was registered? P never installed TextSecure or Signal

Does the WS unregister page work for P? https://whispersystems.org/textsecure/unregister/
If not, P is probably registered with Cyanogenmod's Whisperpush service.

[Post 3]:
How long does P use this number? It could be that the previous owner of the number registered and didn't deregister when the number expired. In that case the unregister link above would also be the answer.

[Post 4]:
After looking into this further, it is true that P had an old phone that used Cyanogenmod. The unregister link @brumsoel sent says "your number is registered with a different server", which suggests that P is indeed register with Cyanogenmod's Whisperpush. AFAICT there is no easy way to unregister from that (see CyanogenMod/android_external_whispersystems_WhisperPush#8), so I guess we're at a dead end for now. I'm closing this since the issue is more with Cyanogenmod than with TextSecure.

[Post 5]:
@cberzan did you try their unregister app? https://plus.google.com/+CyanogenMod/posts/ZdP44tAE9J3
or does it only work on the registered phone itself?

[Post 6]:
Ha! Apparently, there is a website for unregistering. Seems like no one found it yet?
https://whispertool.cyanogenmod.org/ (you'll get a warning about an expired SSL cert, dunno if the thing actually works)

[Post 7]:
I've tried both and they don't seem to work. Anyone have any other suggestions?

[Post 8]:
FYI, someone from CyanogenMod confirmed that https://whispertool.cyanogenmod.org/ is the tool to use for unregistering:
https://twitter.com/CyanogenMod/status/618580635804954624
You may need to wait a couple of hours for your un-registration to propagate to the Whispersystems servers before registering with Textsecure.



## 价值模型参考
以下是可能涉及的价值类型及其定义：
### 人类价值主题 (L2)
- **HV1 (Conformity)**: Adhering to rules, regulations, guidelines, or commonly accepted standards during application use or development.
- **HV2 (Pleasure)**: Providing enjoyment and satisfaction to users when using the application.
- **HV3 (Dignity)**: Maintaining users’ sense of dignity, respect, and personal worth when interacting with the application.
- **HV4 (Inclusiveness)**: Enabling people from different backgrounds, languages, cultures, or knowledge levels to use the application.
- **HV5 (Sense_of_Belonging)**: Supporting users’ feeling of being connected to a group or community through the application.
- **HV6 (Freedom)**: Providing users with options and choices, and avoiding forced dependencies on specific services or workflows.
- **HV7 (Independence)**: Reducing dependency on third-party services or external systems.
- **HV8 (Wealth)**: Considering the monetary or economic cost associated with using the application.
- **HV9 (Privacy)**: Protecting users’ personal information and addressing concerns about data collection or tracking.
- **HV10 (Security)**: Protecting users from external threats or attacks when using the application.

### 系统价值主题 (L3)
- **SV1 (Trust)**: Compelling users to believe that an application is harmless.
- **SV2 (Correctness)**: Ensuring that the application provides expected information and behavior.
- **SV3 (Compatibility)**: Supporting a range of user devices and operating systems.
- **SV4 (Portability)**: Allowing the application to be migrated to other devices.
- **SV5 (Reliability)**: Reducing the occurrence of application failures.
- **SV6 (Efficiency)**: Reducing the time and effort required to perform tasks within the application.
- **SV7 (Energy_Preservation)**: Reducing power or energy consumption during application use.
- **SV8 (Usability)**: Improving the ease of use of the application.
- **SV9 (Accessibility)**: Improving access to information or functionality, particularly for users with special needs.
- **SV10 (Longevity)**: Ensuring prolonged availability of the application in the future.

## 任务
请你以一个软件工程师和项目管理者的视角，回答以下两个问题：

（1）上述Issue讨论是否涉及价值风险？请回答"是"或"否"。

（2）如果涉及价值风险，可能涉及哪些价值风险？请从上述价值模型中选择相关的价值类型，并说明原因。

## 输出格式
请严格按照以下JSON格式输出：
```json
{
  "has_value_risk": true/false,
  "identified_values": [
    {
      "value_id": "价值ID（如HV1, SV1等）",
      "value_name": "价值名称",
      "confidence": 0.0-1.0之间的置信度,
      "reasoning": "识别该价值风险的理由"
    }
  ],
  "overall_analysis": "整体分析说明"
}
```
